subject: a82c503743b088b8e
findings: Disclosed engineering deviation from design D19 (docs/design/2026-07-21-v0-yard-skeleton-design.md:133 names EventSource explicitly): the server heartbeat is a bare SSE comment (board/server/sse.go:75-77) invisible to native EventSource, making the pre-existing gating-matrix disconnect rule (any frame, data or heartbeat, resets the watchdog) unimplementable against that API. Resolved with a raw fetch+ReadableStream SSE reader (board/web/js/sse-protocol.js), tested (node --test) and disclosed in docs/contracts/gating-matrix.md's Disconnect row - flagged as a candidate D19 amendment for the conductor, not a silent substitution. Other disclosures: the wire's fresh freshness variant carries no ageBucketMs (only stale does), so live+fresh renders the word fresh alone rather than the mockup's illustrative fresh, 2s ago, honoring Rule 3 (no client-computed age) over the mock's illustrative text. Visual fidelity implements the binding contracts fully (three registers, verbatim state and outcome words, honesty chrome, bagged and dark dignity distinguished) and the steering direction structurally (track-schematic trains and gates, Solari dense rows for dispatches) but not pixel-perfect mockup polish - system monospace and condensed-sans font stacks instead of the named IBM Plex Mono and Barlow Condensed webfonts, so the board needs no network font fetch. GitNexus MCP tools were not present in this session's toolset, so impact analysis before edits was done by direct code and caller reading rather than the gitnexus_impact tool - disclosed rather than fabricated. docs/setup.md's number 6 quickstart-runner CI trigger was left untouched since its deferral is an owner-level CI-maturity judgment out of this car's scope, though a real, verified quickstart now exists which may inform that decision. No honest-stop was required; no plan-versus-code contradiction blocked any task.
abstract: Car 5 implemented plan tasks 5.1 through 5.5 (docs/plans/2026-07-23-yard-board-plan.md section 6) on branch car/board-car5, base bebfaefac06648e984998cdb13562754067f47f4 verified before editing, five commits ending at 4e5fb8a9ad9fe283406989629965b9a082e73833, nothing pushed. Delivered board/web/ as a vanilla-ESM, no-build-step browser view (compose.js's pure Rules 1 through 4 composition engine with an exhaustive three-axis matrix test; validate.js and ingest.js for wire-schema-validated discard-keeps-last-render with client-side seq ordering; lanes.js and render.js as a pure view-model builder verified directly against a real captured live snapshot fixture; sse-protocol.js as a heartbeat-aware disconnect watchdog; dom-writer.js painting into a hand-rolled minimal DOM shim for Node-level smoke testing; app.js, index.html, and board.css wiring it together) plus board/server changes to serve that view and the wire schema statically, replacing the prior placeholder route. Final state, all observed directly at HEAD: node --test 50 of 50 passed in board/web; go test 59 of 59 passed across all five board/ packages with go vet and gofmt -l both clean; Invoke-Pester on scripts/tests 242 of 242 passed; Car 1's probe-yard-snapshot.mjs printed PROBE RESULT PASS. The README quickstart (cd board then go run ./server) was verified end to end from a clean git clone, not only this worktree, including a real SSE frame and schema validation of the served snapshot. The server was run against this repo's real artifact store, confirmed rendering all five lanes with honest day-one and stale-data states, then killed, with port 4600 confirmed free afterward. The actual human browser observation and screenshot remain the conductor's handback, disclosed honestly rather than fabricated.
